_meta.purpose: Baseline allowlist for JA-94 (pattern-marker presence per grammar example). After authoring data/pattern_markers.json with 99.2% coverage (1768 of 1782 examples), 14 examples remain that don't contain any marker from their parent pattern. Each is a candidate BUG-006-class issue (the example doesn't demonstrate the parent pattern at all) — but cleaning them up requires authored replacements, deferred to a future audit cycle. JA-94 allowlists the 14 baseline cases and trips on any NEW pattern-instance contamination.

classification_legend.BUG-006-CANDIDATE: The example's `ja` doesn't contain any structural marker for the parent pattern. Most likely the example was incorrectly filed under this pattern; the right fix is to either (a) replace the example with one that demonstrates the parent pattern, or (b) move the example to its correct pattern (if it demonstrates a different pattern's structure).

_meta.next_review_trigger: Any grammar.json edit that adds new examples. JA-94 will trip if a new example doesn't contain ≥1 marker. Either (a) the new example is a legitimate demonstration via a non-canonical form → expand the pattern's markers in data/pattern_markers.json, or (b) the new example is BUG-006-class wrong → fix the example.

baseline_failing_examples.ja: ごりょうしんは おげんきですか。
baseline_failing_examples.note: Parent pattern 'nominalizer in basic use' but example uses honorific お+adj+です, not a nominalizer. Possibly mis-filed; the example fits Honorifics or Adjectives instead.

baseline_failing_examples.ja: そふは げんきです。
baseline_failing_examples.note: Same class as ex[4] — no nominalizer pattern demonstrated.

baseline_failing_examples.ja: おばさんは 花が すきです。
baseline_failing_examples.note: Same class — no nominalizer; this is a が-particle preference statement.

baseline_failing_examples.ja: ここは としょかんです。
baseline_failing_examples.note: Parent pattern is どこ but example uses ここ (a different demonstrative). Mis-filed; belongs under n5-016 / n5-041 (ここ-series).

baseline_failing_examples.ja: そこに ねこが います。
baseline_failing_examples.note: Same — example uses そこ, not どこ.

baseline_failing_examples.ja: だれが きましたか。
baseline_failing_examples.note: Same — example uses だれ (a different question word). Belongs under n5-018 / n5-046 (だれ patterns).

baseline_failing_examples.ja: わたしは ねこを かいません。
baseline_failing_examples.note: Parent pattern Verb-る / Verb-う (plain dictionary form) but example uses polite negative かいません. Wrong example; should demonstrate plain dictionary form.
Parent pattern Verb-てください but example uses noun+を+ください alone (no V-te). Belongs under the 〜をください pattern (n5-149).

baseline_failing_examples.ja: 日曜日に へやを そうじします。
baseline_failing_examples.note: Parent pattern な-Adjective + な + Noun but example has no na-adjective. Mis-filed.

baseline_failing_examples.ja: ごじはん から いちじかん べんきょうしました。
baseline_failing_examples.note: Parent pattern 〜ふん/ぷん (minutes) but example uses じはん (5:30) and じかん (1 hour), no ふん/ぷん. Edge case — uses related time units. Could fit n5-113 (〜じはん) or n5-122 (〜じかん).

baseline_failing_examples.ja: おなかが すいたから、なにか たべましょう。
baseline_failing_examples.note: Parent pattern 〜でしょう but example uses volitional たべましょう (a different ましょう pattern). Mis-filed; belongs under n5-071 (volitional ましょう).

baseline_failing_examples.ja: 四時に あいましょう。
baseline_failing_examples.note: Same class — uses volitional ましょう, not probability でしょう.
Same — volitional ましょうか, not probability でしょう.

baseline_failing_examples.ja: ホテルは どこに ありますか。
baseline_failing_examples.note: Parent pattern 〜さん (honorific suffix) but example has no さん. Mis-filed; belongs under existence-with-ある (n5-089) or どこ (n5-048).

_audit_summary.follow_on_target: A future audit cycle should walk this list and either replace each example with a proper pattern-demonstrating one, or move it to the correct parent pattern. Until then, JA-94 allowlists them — no NEW pattern-instance contamination can land without tripping CI.
